perturbation.original_text: The Professional agrees to use only the golf clubs and golf bags of the Company in any golf event, whether professional or social, during the Term. The Professional agrees (i) to use no golf bag bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company and (ii) to wear no apparel bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company, and will prohibit any caddy of hers from bearing any such identification.
perturbation.changed_text: The Professional agrees to use only the golf clubs and golf bags of the Company in any golf event, whether professional or social, during the Term. However, the Professional is only encouraged to use no golf bag bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company and (ii) to wear no apparel bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company, and will encourage any caddy of hers from bearing any such identification.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the exclusive use of the Company's golf clubs and bags, while the modified version uses 'encouraged' instead of 'agrees' for the golf bag and apparel, making the first line in the original text contradictory and uncertain whether exclusivity is legally required or just requested. This creates an in-text contradiction in the same paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional agrees to use only the golf clubs and golf bags of the Company in any golf event, whether professional or social, during the Term. However, the Professional is only encouraged to use no golf bag bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company and (ii) to wear no apparel bearing any identification of a competitor of the Company, and will encourage any caddy of hers from bearing any such identification.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction by weakening the obligation regarding golf bags, apparel, and caddy identification from a mandatory agreement ('agrees') to a mere encouragement ('encouraged'). The original text established a firm requirement for exclusive use of the Company's equipment and branding, while the changed text creates ambiguity, making it uncertain whether compliance with the golf bag, apparel, and caddy identification restrictions is legally required or merely suggested. This directly contradicts the initial strong statement of exclusivity regarding golf clubs and bags, triggering an in-text contradiction. The original text is altered from "The Professional agrees" to "the Professional is only encouraged to use". Since both portions are located in the same paragraph, that constitutes a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will reimburse the Professional for her reasonable and necessary travel expenses in connection with her performance of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may, at its sole discretion, reimburse the Professional for some travel expenses in connection with her performance of the Services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Company 'will reimburse' implying a mandatory action. The modified version changes this to 'may, at its sole discretion, reimburse' implying a discretionary action. This contradicts the expectation set in Section 4.4 (the original text) and creates uncertainty about the Professional's entitlement to expense reimbursement, contradicting the text in section 4.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional acknowledges that the Company is under no obligation to create or maintain the Products. The Professional agrees that payment of the Base Fee shall satisfy all obligations of the Company hereunder if it elects not to create or market and sell the Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a mandatory reimbursement, while the changed text introduces a discretionary reimbursement. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the Professional's entitlement to travel expenses, contradicting section 4.1 (although section 4.1 is not given in the prompt). However, the contradicted text describes the Company's obligation to create the products. There is no contradiction, because both texts do not relate to one another, or are conflicting. The fact that the reimbursement is optional does not affect the obligation of the company to create or maintain the products.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall, therefore, be entitled to obtain an injunction against the Professional restraining any violation, further violation, or threatened violation of Section 9 above, in addition to any other remedies to which the Company may be entitled by law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company might be allowed to pursue an injunction against the Professional restraining any violation, further violation, or threatened violation of Section 9 above, in addition to any other remedies to which the Company may seek by law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the 'Company shall... be entitled to obtain an injunction', indicating a guaranteed right. The modified text changes this to 'Company might be allowed to pursue an injunction' changing 'entitled' to 'allowed' weakens it and makes it uncertain whether the company can obtain an injunction. This contradicts the original certainty established in Section 10.1 (the original text).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Professional acknowledges and agrees that a breach of the covenants contained in Section 9 of this Agreement would actually or potentially deprive the Company of a substantial amount of sales and business value and that the amount of injury would be impossible or difficult to ascertain fully. The Company might be allowed to pursue an injunction against the Professional restraining any violation, further violation, or threatened violation of Section 9 above, in addition to any other remedies to which the Company may seek by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Company 'shall be entitled to obtain an injunction'. The changed text weakens this right to 'might be allowed to pursue an injunction'. This introduces uncertainty regarding the Company's ability to obtain an injunction, directly contradicting the original certainty and creating a conflict with the contradicted text. The change makes it uncertain whether the Company can obtain an injunction, conflicting with the original certainty established in Section 10.1 (the original text) and further contradicting the contradicted text because the company is not guaranteed an injunction. This also directly contradicts the contradicted text because the change creates an in-text contradiction, as the two sections now conflict regarding the Company's right to pursue an injunction.
